programmes.show_description: How do they make Tennessee whiskey the traditional Lincoln County way? How do night goggles work? How do the Finnish relax in 160-degree saunas?
How do they grow enough vanilla to make the world's favourite ice cream flavour? How are anchors for huge ships built? How are 12 million lighters made every year?
Deepak's Dream Porsche Cayenne. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley help police officer Deepak trade up his Nissan Juke to a stylish Porsche Cayenne.
The Special. Mike and Elvis deliver the ultimate guide to buying a dream car, featuring auctions, car shows, auto jumbles and Mike's very own forecourt.
Suffolk. Henry and Sam are in Suffolk where they find a barn filled with Porsche tractors, Ducati pushbikes and many more rare and surprising vehicles.
Witney. Henry and Sam head to Witney where they meet Lez, a model-builder extraordinaire, and come away with an unusual challenge. Can they make a tidy profit for Lez's trike?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as gummy vitamins and boat trailers made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as water well cylinders and nonconductive tools made?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Metal worker Dom is tasked with repairing an old barber's pole. Lucia restores a faded artwork, while Will transforms a wooden writing case.
Drew and Tee visit Newark, a town renowned for its antique connections. Drew finds a set of Regency bed steps and a genuine, very rare 18th century mirror.
Drew and Tee visit Insect Circus in Suffolk, where they discover a giant pair of 20th-century stage shoes and scratch-made models of fairground rides.
Vern and Marko are at crisis point and Rick takes to the air in a race to peg a new lease. Plus, Alex hunts down poachers before a sunset wedding on his Quartz Hill lease.
The Dirt Dogs face the fallout after Marko's shock departure, but they fight back with big tech. Plus, Rick launches a solo rescue mission for a rookie prospector.
In Yorkshire, Roo learns about Victorian loos while David discovers an exciting vintage watch. Later, two brooches and a wooden cat go under the gavel in Dundee.
Angus and the team clear out a big house in Whixall, Shropshire. They uncover a Victorian desk set, a hanging lantern and Vogue clothing patterns.
Metal worker Dom is tasked with repairing an old barber's pole. Lucia restores a faded artwork, while Will transforms a wooden writing case.
Stormy Weather, Hidden Treasure. At a wind-swept farm on a hill top in Cornwall, Henry and Sam find a collection of cars and tractors and a very rare German scooter.
Hot Rods, Trucks And Miniature Bikes. In East Sussex, Henry and Sam visit a dedicated collector of American vehicles. His collection includes hot rods, trucks and other quirky items.
Theo And Sarah. Henry and Fuzz meet a lifelong biker with unique motorbikes. Fuzz discovers a 1950s step through scooter, but Henry prefers low-capacity sports bikes.
Adam And Dan. Henry and stand-in searcher Guy are in Wiltshire visiting collectors Adam and Dan who have an assortment of trucks, tractors, and rare American cars.
Jugtastic! Angus visits a home with a pub in it, filled with over 160 jugs. Later, he encounters a colossal collection of Glen Millar Vinyl and V-discs!
Desperately Needing Angus. Angus helps a woman fund her elderly mum's care, and he clears a home for a couple who went through a life-changing, traumatic event on holiday.
A Superstar DJ. Catherine Southon and Phil Serrell breeze around Essex, picking up curiosities in a Mercedes convertible. Catherine enjoys a revenge prank on Phil.
Antiques experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriot hit the road in Suffolk and Norfolk. They find a 19th century automaton and a stoneware Doulton flask.
A Victorian Bonanza. Angus takes a trip back in time when he visits a home that's dressed like a Victorian townhouse. Later, he helps a mother and daughter with an emotional task.
Sacred Secret & Saucy Surprise. Angus sells the treasures of an Abbey to fund the monks' retirement, and a motoring fanatic's cheeky sculpture sets pulses racing on sale day.
Susannah Constantine & Leslie Ash. Angus raids TV presenter Susannah's couture stash, then helps actress Leslie uncover treasures, memories, and a few famous surprises!
How do they craft footballs for the National Football League? How do they farm mussels? How do they design the ultimate fighter pilot's helmet?
How are powerful Scania trucks built? How do they use the world's biggest DNA database to solve crime? How is paraffin used to make Gouda cheese?
How do they make the finest classical guitars? How do they keep the blades turning on a wind turbine? How do they produce America's ultimate mowing machines?
How can lifeboats hold up to 150 people? How can mind control techniques help golfers improve their shots? How do they make millions of celebratory stamps?
Aaron's Audi Q7. Mike and Elvis try to trade their way to a dream Audi Q7 for engineer Aaron. He's recently become a father and would love to drive to visit family in Hungary.
Marc's Caterham. NHS worker Marc suffers from a degenerative condition and doesn't know how long he'll be well enough to drive. The boys race to get him a Caterham sports car.
Stormy Weather, Hidden Treasure. At a wind-swept farm on a hill top in Cornwall, Henry and Sam find a collection of cars and tractors and a very rare German scooter.
Hot Rods, Trucks And Miniature Bikes. In East Sussex, Henry and Sam visit a dedicated collector of American vehicles. His collection includes hot rods, trucks and other quirky items.
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as stand-up paddleboards and vacuum cleaners made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as detectable warning panels and model Stirling engines made?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Mechanical expert Tim repairs an antique wooden phonograph. Sonnaz works on an unusual armchair, while Steve revamps an old miner's lamp.
Drew and Tee explore three new territories. An unusual set of stained-glass windows, an 1840s lamp and a retro Japanese toy police pique Drew's interest.
In the Cotswolds, Drew is given exclusive access to a dozen shipping containers. Drew picks out three items, including a 19th century glazed bookcase.
Scorching 45-degree desert heat pushes Rick to his limit. Alex risks facing snakes in his hunt for gold-rich pay dirt, and Greg crosses Australia to care for his sick father.
The Dirt Dogs and Gold Gypsies fight back against the rising number of gold thieves. Meanwhile, the Scrappers' washplant conveyor belt comes to a screaming halt.
Roo's head gets stuck in an antique while David buys a bull and an elephant. An eventful trip in Scotland ends with ice cream and an Edinburgh auction.
Angus is in Newcastle to clear out a home that's full of books. He takes an interest in a Bechstein piano, a scary vase and a silver loving cup.
Mechanical expert Tim repairs an antique wooden phonograph. Sonnaz works on an unusual armchair, while Steve revamps an old miner's lamp.
Everything But The Kitchen Sink. In Cornwall, Henry and Sam visit a trials car enthusiast with an eclectic set of sheds. Will they come away with a jeep, a bike or something else?
A Right Royal Profit? Henry and Sam are in Cornwall visiting the Queen's armourer, Terry English, and his amazing collection of vintage cars, bikes and engines. What will they find?
Ross. Henry and Fuzz are in Surrey visiting classic cars and tractor collector Ross. Will they go for a gangster-style Rover or a 1920s Renault saloon?
Nick And Hollie. Henry and Fuzz return to Surrey to visit collectors Nick and Hollie, who have an incredible array of cars including a 1921 Rolls Royce Silver Ghost.
Clocked & Loaded Auction. Angus helps a roofer-turned-artist sell his sculptures for a small fortune. Plus, a woman is auctioning off her inherited clock collection.
Heavy Metal Sputnik. Angus helps two grieving families. Satellite-themed cup-holders and a huge gold ring do well, but a haul of hard rock albums gets everyone spinning.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Andy And Tim / Petrol Pump. Henry and Fuzz head for Suffolk to rummage through Andy and Tim's collection of vintage vehicles, eccentric oddities and exquisite automobilia.
Mandy / Ariel Motorbike. Henry and Sam go to Hull to meet Mandy, whose late husband was an avid collector of classic motorbikes. There are several rare Vincents and Ariels on offer.
Debts To Settle. The Blacklighters are pushing for a big pay day. With weather threatening to halt their operation, the Misfits have to get crafty with their setup.
American Dream. The Cogs bid for car-show glory with an iconic American pick-up truck. Richard is back on the racetrack. Can he impress the classic car power-players?
How do they produce tequila from cactus plants in Mexico? How does New York City run on steam? How do they produce soft ice cream?
How do stuntmen survive a bad day at work? How do they mine precious amber gemstones? How do they build Seabreacher watercraft that move like dolphins?
